heading: Voluntary flexible agreements with guaranty agencies
label: 1078–1 Voluntary flexible agreements with guaranty agencies

children.label: A
children.text: any statutory requirement pertaining to the terms and conditions attached to student loans or default claim payments made to lenders;

children.label: B
children.text: the prohibitions on inducements contained in ; orsection 1078(b)(3) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the Federal default fee required by and the second sentence of .section 1078(b)(1)(H) of this titlesection 1078–8(h) of this title

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 2 Eligibility
children.text: During fiscal years 1999, 2000, and 2001, the Secretary may enter into a voluntary, flexible agreement with not more than 6 guaranty agencies that had 1 or more agreements with the Secretary under subsections (b) and (c) of as of the day before . Beginning in fiscal year 2002, any guaranty agency or consortium thereof may enter into a voluntary flexible agreement with the Secretary.section 1078 of this titleOctober 7, 1998

children.label: i
children.text: program integrity and program and cost efficiencies, delinquency prevention, and default aversion, including a comparison of such outcomes to such outcomes for each guaranty agency operating under an agreement under subsection (b) or (c) of ;section 1078 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: consumer education programs described in ; andsection 1083a of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the availability and delivery of student financial aid.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of each voluntary flexible agreement and the performance goals established by the Secretary for each agreement;

children.label: I
children.text: guaranty agencies operating under voluntary flexible agreements;

children.label: II
children.text: the specific statutory or regulatory waivers provided to each such guaranty agency; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other waivers provided to other guaranty agencies under paragraph (1);

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the standards by which each guaranty agency’s performance under the guaranty agency’s voluntary flexible agreement was assessed and the degree to which each guaranty agency achieved the performance standards;

children.label: iv
children.text: an analysis of the fees paid by the Secretary, and the costs and efficiencies achieved under each voluntary flexible agreement; and

children.label: v
children.text: an identification of promising practices for program improvement that could be replicated by other guaranty agencies.
shall be developed by the Secretary, in consultation with the guaranty agency, on a case-by-case basis;

children.label: i
children.text: administering the issuance of insurance on loans made under this part on behalf of the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: monitoring insurance commitments made under this part;

children.label: iii
children.text: default aversion activities;

children.label: iv
children.text: review of default claims made by lenders;

children.label: v
children.text: payment of default claims;

children.label: vi
children.text: collection of defaulted loans;

children.label: vii
children.text: adoption of internal systems of accounting and auditing that are acceptable to the Secretary, and reporting the result thereof to the Secretary in a timely manner, and on an accurate, and auditable basis;

children.label: viii
children.text: timely and accurate collection and reporting of such other data as the Secretary may require to carry out the purposes of the programs under this subchapter;

children.label: ix
children.text: monitoring of institutions and lenders participating in the program under this part; and

children.label: x
children.text: informational outreach to schools and students in support of access to higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: regarding the fees the Secretary shall pay, in lieu of revenues that the guaranty agency may otherwise receive under this part, to the guaranty agency under the agreement, and other funds that the guaranty agency may receive or retain under the agreement, except that in no case may the cost to the Secretary of the agreement, as reasonably projected by the Secretary, exceed the cost to the Secretary, as similarly projected, in the absence of the agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: regarding the use of net revenues, as described in the agreement under this section, for such other activities in support of postsecondary education as may be agreed to by the Secretary and the guaranty agency;

children.label: D
children.text: regarding the standards by which the guaranty agency’s performance of the agency’s responsibilities under the agreement will be assessed, and the consequences for a guaranty agency’s failure to achieve a specified level of performance on 1 or more performance standards;

children.label: E
children.text: regarding the circumstances in which a guaranty agency’s agreement under this section may be ended in advance of the agreement’s expiration date;

children.label: F
children.text: regarding such other businesses, previously purchased or developed with reserve funds, that relate to the program under this part and in which the Secretary permits the guaranty agency to engage; and

children.label: G
children.text: such other provisions as the Secretary may determine to be necessary to protect the United States from the risk of unreasonable loss and to promote the purposes of this part;
shall provide for uniform lender participation with the guaranty agency under the terms of the agreement; and
shall not prohibit or restrict borrowers from selecting a lender of the borrower’s choosing, subject to the prohibitions and restrictions applicable to the selection under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: an invitation for the guaranty agencies to enter into agreements under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the criteria that the Secretary will use for selecting the guaranty agencies with which the Secretary will enter into agreements under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the voluntary flexible agreement and the performance goals established by the Secretary for the agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: a list of participating guaranty agencies and the specific statutory or regulatory waivers provided to each guaranty agency;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the standards by which each guaranty agency’s performance under the agreement will be assessed; and

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the fees that will be paid to each participating guaranty agency.

children.heading: Waiver notice
children.label: 3 Waiver notice
children.text: The Secretary shall notify the members of the authorizing committees not later than 30 days prior to the granting of a waiver pursuant to subsection (a)(2) to a guaranty agency that is not a party to a voluntary flexible agreement.1

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 4 Public availability
children.text: The text of any voluntary flexible agreement, and any subsequent revisions, and any waivers related to that are not part of such an agreement, shall be readily available to the public.section 1078(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Modification notice
children.label: 5 Modification notice
children.text: The Secretary shall notify the members of the authorizing committees 30 days prior to any modifications to an agreement under this section.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: d Termination
children.text: At the expiration or early termination of an agreement under this section, the Secretary shall reinstate the guaranty agency’s prior agreements under subsections (b) and (c) of , subject only to such additional requirements as the Secretary determines to be necessary in order to ensure the efficient transfer of responsibilities between the agreement under this section and the agreements under subsections (b) and (c) of , and including the guaranty agency’s compliance with reserve requirements under sections 1072 and 1078 of this title.section 1078 of this titlesection 1078 of this title
